bench_clear_evidence: Steven stated 'bench clear' on 2026-05-17 for the exact bounded-shaped PIDFF micro-profile command: profile=bounded-shaped-pidff-micro-profile, strategy=pidff-bounded-effect, max-percent=5, duration-ms=2000, movement-threshold-degrees=1.
ffb_mode_review_evidence: No-output readiness at 2026-05-17T05:01:59Z confirmed native_control_blocking_items=[] and native_actuator_response_proven=true; prior response receipt proved PIDFF writes and Stop All cleanup while avoiding direct 0x20/high torque/serial/firmware/DFU; no topology, power, or wheelbase-mode change was reported before this bench-clear.

candidate_profiles.name: repeat_current_profile_after_specific_setup_fix
candidate_profiles.use_only_if: Bench, setup, or FFB-mode review identifies a specific reason the previous 5 percent response stayed below the visible threshold.

candidate_profiles.name: higher_or_longer_profile
candidate_profiles.reason: The current visible smoke command remains capped at 5 percent and 2000 ms; any higher or longer profile needs a separate software and safety plan.

intentional_profile_design.objective: Record the next visible-motion profile decision before any output command is authorized.

expected_small_angle_envelope_degrees.basis: operator-visible target for the next reviewed command; this is not a pass claim and not an authorization to run output

profile_requirements: use only descriptor-proven PIDFF bounded-effect reports
record exact force, duration, pulse shape, return or cleanup behavior, and expected angle envelope before hardware output
require final PIDFF Stop All cleanup
preserve the existing response/visible-motion receipt before any replacement
require a fresh bench-clear for the exact reviewed command
selected_next_profile.reason_previous_5_percent: The 5 percent / 2000 ms PIDFF command produced actuator response above the response floor, so the next profile should target a small visible steering envelope instead of treating the prior run as dead output.
selected_next_profile.reviewed_command_template: wheelctl moza actuator-visible-smoke --device <r5> --lane ci/hardware/moza-r5/<date> --prior-actuator-proof ci/hardware/moza-r5/<date>/native-actuator-profile-smoke.json --steering-proof ci/hardware/moza-r5/<date>/steering-angle-stream-proof.json --profile bounded-shaped-pidff-micro-profile --strategy pidff-bounded-effect --max-percent 5 --duration-ms 2000 --movement-threshold-degrees 1 --confirm-actuator-visible --json-out ci/hardware/moza-r5/<date>/native-actuator-visible-smoke.json --json

software_next_step.description: The bounded shaped PIDFF micro-profile is available for dry-run/preflight; use wheelctl moza authorize-visible-output to record fresh bench-clear, preserve the response-only receipt, and authorize exactly one matching hardware-output command.

notes: Authorization artifact only: this file is not evidence that visible motion passed.
The exact 5 percent / 2000 ms bounded-shaped PIDFF micro-profile authorization was consumed by native-actuator-visible-smoke.json.
The consumed run kept direct report 0x20, high torque, serial config, firmware, and DFU disabled and sent final PIDFF Stop All cleanup.
No further hardware-output command is authorized by this artifact; do not rerun, lengthen the profile, or attempt a controlled-angle move without a separate reviewed plan and fresh bench-clear.

required_before_next_output.evidence: The prior bench-clear was consumed by the 5 percent / 2000 ms bounded-shaped PIDFF micro-profile attempt. A new exact-command bench-clear is required after a separate plan names the next output command.

The 2026-05-17 bounded-shaped PIDFF micro-profile produced the same 0.181 degree measured delta with successful writes and Stop All cleanup; inspect wheelbase/vendor FFB state before authorizing another output attempt.
steering-angle-stream-proof.json passed before the failed run; refresh if USB, power, topology, wheelbase mode, or bench setup changed.
Preserved current response-only receipt at ci/hardware/moza-r5/2026-05-13\native-actuator-visible-smoke-response-only.json before authorizing replacement.

The prior bench-clear was consumed by the 5 percent / 2000 ms bounded-shaped PIDFF micro-profile attempt. A new exact-command bench-clear is required after a separate plan names the next output command.

Steven proposed 5 percent / 3000 ms and asked about 5 percent / 30000 ms after the failed 2000 ms attempt, but both exceed the current reviewed cap and are not authorized without a separate software and safety plan.
Steven named 90 degrees right and 90 degrees left to reset as the initial visible target. That is a later feedback-bounded angle profile, not a dwell extension of the current visible-smoke command.

response_classification.interpretation: The 5 percent / 2000 ms PIDFF run produced measurable steering response above the response floor, but it did not meet the operator-visible motion threshold.

last_output_attempt.command: wheelctl moza actuator-visible-smoke --device hid-0x346E-0x0004-if2-0x0001-0x0004 --lane ci/hardware/moza-r5/2026-05-13 --prior-actuator-proof ci/hardware/moza-r5/2026-05-13\native-actuator-profile-smoke.json --steering-proof ci/hardware/moza-r5/2026-05-13\steering-angle-stream-proof.json --profile bounded-shaped-pidff-micro-profile --strategy pidff-bounded-effect --max-percent 5 --duration-ms 2000 --movement-threshold-degrees 1 --confirm-actuator-visible --json-out ci/hardware/moza-r5/2026-05-13\native-actuator-visible-smoke.json --json

proposed_follow_up_profile.reason: The current visible smoke command and reviewed plan are capped at 5 percent and 2000 ms. A 3000 ms output profile is longer than the reviewed cap and must be planned separately before any hardware output.
safety_constraints: fresh bench-clear for the exact reviewed command

proposed_follow_up_profiles.reason: Three seconds is longer than the reviewed 2000 ms cap and still uses open-loop dwell semantics.
Thirty seconds is a sustained force dwell, not an angle-bounded visible-motion proof.

proposed_follow_up_profiles.target: 90 degrees right, then 90 degrees left to reset
proposed_follow_up_profiles.reason: A 90 degree right/left target should be implemented as a feedback-bounded angle profile with steering-sample stop guards, return-to-start cleanup, and staged smaller-angle receipts first.
